case_title: HENRY BODDEN JR. VS. UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA AT SAN FRANCISCO et al

docket_entries.description: OPPOSITION TO DEMURRER FILED BY PLAINTIFF BODDEN JR., HENRY
MINI-MINUTES FOR JUN-04-2010 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

MALPRACTICE - MEDICAL/DENTAL, COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF BODDEN JR., HENRY AS TO DEFENDANT UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA AT SAN FRANCISCO REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA SAN FRANCISCO GENERAL HOSPITAL CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DOES 1 THRU 20, INCL. SUMMONS ISSUED, JUDICIAL COUNCIL CIVIL CASE COVER SHEET FILED CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE SCHEDULED FOR SEP-03-2010 PROOF OF SERVICE DUE ON                  JUN-01-2010 CASE MANAGEMENT STATEMENT DUE ON         AUG-19-2010

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION (CIVIL BATTERY.) PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PLEAD A DELIBERATE INTENT TO DEVIATE FROM THE CONSENT GIVEN. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECT CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO REMAINING CAUSES OF ACTION. IN 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FRAUD WITH SPECIFICITY, DETRIMENTAL RELIANCE AND RESULTING HARM. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED THAT DEFENDANTS WERE AWARE THAT THE DRILL BIT WAS IN PLAINTIFF'S SKULL. TO SUPPORT THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD POSITIVE ASSERTIONS AND ACTUAL REPRESENTATIONS BY DEFENDANTS. IN 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION, FRAUDULENT CONCEALMENT MUST BE PLED WITH SPECIFICITY, AND THAT DEFENDANTS' CONCEALMENT ALTERED PLAINTIFF'S POST-OPERATIVE COURSE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-07-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, Defendant City and County of San Francisco Demurrer to complaint. Appearances: Kristen A. Pico, Esq. of Hassard Bonnington LLP (415) 288-9800 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to 2nd cause of action (Civil Battery.) Plaintiff failed to plead a deliberate intent to deviate from the consent given. No reasonable probability that defect can be cured by amendment. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to remaining causes of action. In 3rd cause of action, plaintiff must plead fraud with specificity, detrimental reliance and resulting harm. Plaintiff has not pled that defendants were aware that the drill bit was in plaintiff's skull. To support the 4th cause of action, plaintiff must plead positive assertions and actual representations by defendants. In 5th cause of action, fraudulent concealment must be pled with specificity, and that defendants' concealment altered plaintiff's post-operative course. Order signed in open Court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
